CreateMicrovmAuthToken.name: CreateMicrovmAuthToken
CreateMicrovmAuthToken.documentation: Creates an authentication token for accessing a running MicroVM. The token grants access to the specified ports on the MicroVM endpoint.

CreateMicrovmImage.name: CreateMicrovmImage
CreateMicrovmImage.documentation: Creates a MicroVM image from the specified code artifact and base image. The build is asynchronous — the image transitions from CREATING to CREATED on success, or CREATE_FAILED on failure. Use GetMicrovmImage to poll for completion.

CreateMicrovmShellAuthToken.name: CreateMicrovmShellAuthToken
CreateMicrovmShellAuthToken.documentation: Creates a shell authentication token for interactive shell access to a running MicroVM. The MicroVM must have been run with the SHELL_INGRESS network connector attached.

DeleteMicrovmImage.name: DeleteMicrovmImage
DeleteMicrovmImage.documentation: Deletes a MicroVM image. This operation is idempotent; deleting an image that has already been deleted succeeds without error.

DeleteMicrovmImageVersion.name: DeleteMicrovmImageVersion
DeleteMicrovmImageVersion.documentation: Deletes a specific version of a MicroVM image. This operation is idempotent; deleting a version that has already been deleted succeeds without error.

GetMicrovm.name: GetMicrovm
GetMicrovm.documentation: Retrieves the details of a specific MicroVM, including its state, endpoint, image information, and configuration. The state field is eventually consistent — determine readiness by connecting to the endpoint.

GetMicrovmImage.name: GetMicrovmImage
GetMicrovmImage.documentation: Retrieves the details of a MicroVM image, including its state, versions, and configuration.

GetMicrovmImageBuild.name: GetMicrovmImageBuild
GetMicrovmImageBuild.documentation: Retrieves the details of a specific MicroVM image build, including its state, target architecture, and snapshot information.

GetMicrovmImageVersion.name: GetMicrovmImageVersion
GetMicrovmImageVersion.documentation: Retrieves the details of a specific version of a MicroVM image, including its configuration, state, and build information.

ListManagedMicrovmImageVersions.name: ListManagedMicrovmImageVersions
ListManagedMicrovmImageVersions.documentation: Lists versions of a managed MicroVM image. We recommend using pagination to ensure that the operation returns quickly and successfully.

ListManagedMicrovmImages.name: ListManagedMicrovmImages
ListManagedMicrovmImages.documentation: Lists AWS managed MicroVM images available for use as base images. We recommend using pagination to ensure that the operation returns quickly and successfully.

ListMicrovmImageBuilds.name: ListMicrovmImageBuilds
ListMicrovmImageBuilds.documentation: Lists builds for a MicroVM image version with optional filtering by architecture and chipset. We recommend using pagination to ensure that the operation returns quickly and successfully.

ListMicrovmImageVersions.name: ListMicrovmImageVersions
ListMicrovmImageVersions.documentation: Lists versions of a MicroVM image. We recommend using pagination to ensure that the operation returns quickly and successfully.

ListMicrovmImages.name: ListMicrovmImages
ListMicrovmImages.documentation: Lists MicroVM images in the account with optional name filtering. We recommend using pagination to ensure that the operation returns quickly and successfully.

ListMicrovms.name: ListMicrovms
ListMicrovms.documentation: Lists MicroVMs in the account with optional filtering by image and version. We recommend using pagination to ensure that the operation returns quickly and successfully.

ListTags.name: ListTags
ListTags.documentation: Lists the tags associated with a Lambda MicroVM resource.

ResumeMicrovm.name: ResumeMicrovm
ResumeMicrovm.documentation: Resumes a suspended MicroVM, restoring it to RUNNING state with all state intact. The MicroVM must be in SUSPENDED state.

RunMicrovm.name: RunMicrovm
RunMicrovm.documentation: Runs a new MicroVM from the specified image. The MicroVM starts in PENDING state and transitions to RUNNING once provisioning completes. To connect, generate an authentication token using CreateMicrovmAuthToken.

SuspendMicrovm.name: SuspendMicrovm
SuspendMicrovm.documentation: Suspends a running MicroVM, preserving its full memory and disk state. The MicroVM transitions through SUSPENDING to SUSPENDED. To restore, call ResumeMicrovm or send traffic to the endpoint if autoResumeEnabled is true.

TagResource.name: TagResource
TagResource.documentation: Adds tags to a Lambda MicroVM resource.

TerminateMicrovm.name: TerminateMicrovm
TerminateMicrovm.documentation: Terminates a MicroVM. This operation is idempotent; terminating a MicroVM that has already been terminated succeeds without error.

UntagResource.name: UntagResource
UntagResource.documentation: Removes tags from a Lambda MicroVM resource.

UpdateMicrovmImage.name: UpdateMicrovmImage
UpdateMicrovmImage.documentation: Updates the configuration of a MicroVM image and triggers a new version build. This operation uses PUT semantics — all required fields (codeArtifact, baseImageArn, buildRoleArn) must be provided with every request.

UpdateMicrovmImageVersion.name: UpdateMicrovmImageVersion
UpdateMicrovmImageVersion.documentation: Updates the status of a specific MicroVM image version.

AccessDeniedException.documentation: You do not have sufficient access to perform this action.

Capability.documentation: Capability granted to the application when booted

CapabilityList.documentation: List of capabilities granted to the application when booted

logGroup.documentation: The name of the CloudWatch Logs log group to send logs to.

logStream.documentation: The name of the CloudWatch Logs log stream within the log group.

uri.documentation: The URI of the code artifact, such as an Amazon S3 path or Amazon ECR image URI.

CodeArtifact.documentation: Contains the location of the code artifact for a MicroVM image.

resourceId.documentation: The identifier of the resource that caused the conflict.

resourceType.documentation: The type of the resource that caused the conflict.

ConflictException.documentation: The request could not be completed due to a conflict with the current state of the resource.

CpuConfiguration.documentation: Configuration for the CPU architecture of a MicroVM.

microvmIdentifier.documentation: The ID of the MicroVM to create an authentication token for.

expirationInMinutes.documentation: The duration in minutes before the authentication token expires. Maximum: 60 minutes.

allowedPorts.documentation: The list of port specifications that the authentication token grants access to on the MicroVM.

authToken.documentation: A map containing the authentication token. Use the value at key "X-aws-proxy-auth" as the header value when connecting to the MicroVM endpoint.

baseImageArn.documentation: The ARN of the Lambda-managed base MicroVM image to build upon. Use ListManagedMicrovmImages to discover available base images.

baseImageVersion.documentation: The specific version of the base MicroVM image to use.

buildRoleArn.documentation: The ARN of the IAM role assumed during the image build process. This role must have permissions to access the code artifact and any required resources.

codeArtifact.documentation: The code artifact containing the application code and metadata for the MicroVM image.

logging.documentation: The logging configuration for build-time and runtime logs. Specify {"cloudWatch": {"logGroup": "..."}} to stream logs to a custom CloudWatch log group, or {"disabled": {}} to turn off logging.

egressNetworkConnectors.documentation: The list of egress network connectors available to the MicroVM at runtime.

cpuConfigurations.documentation: The list of supported CPU configurations for the MicroVM.

additionalOsCapabilities.documentation: Additional OS capabilities granted to the MicroVM runtime environment.

environmentVariables.documentation: Environment variables set in the MicroVM runtime environment.

name.documentation: The name of the MicroVM image. Must be unique within the AWS account.

tags.documentation: A set of key-value pairs that you can attach to the resource. Use tags to categorize resources for cost allocation, access control (ABAC), and organization.

clientToken.documentation: A unique, case-sensitive identifier you provide to ensure the idempotency of the request. If you retry a request that completed successfully using the same client token, the operation returns the successful response without performing any further actions.

imageArn.documentation: The ARN of the created MicroVM image.

state.documentation: The current state of the MicroVM image.

latestFailedImageVersion.documentation: The latest failed version of the MicroVM image, if any.

maxIdleDurationSeconds.documentation: The maximum time in seconds that a MicroVM can remain idle before it is automatically suspended.

suspendedDurationSeconds.documentation: The maximum time in seconds that a MicroVM can remain suspended before it is automatically terminated.

autoResumeEnabled.documentation: Indicates whether the MicroVM automatically resumes when it receives a request while suspended.

IdlePolicy.documentation: Configuration that controls MicroVM auto-suspend and auto-resume behavior. Idle time is measured by inbound traffic through the MicroVM proxy endpoint — if no requests arrive within the configured duration, the MicroVM is suspended.

retryAfterSeconds.documentation: The number of seconds to wait before retrying the request.

InternalServerException.documentation: An internal server error occurred. Retry the request later.

InvalidParameterValueException.documentation: One of the parameters in the request is not valid.

LoggingDisabled.documentation: Specifies that logging is disabled for the MicroVM.

documentation: Provides APIs to create, manage, and operate AWS Lambda MicroVMs and their associated MicroVM Image environments.
